description: A dark, high-density, multi-syllabic rap piece inspired by Eminem, billy woods, clipping., and Dälek
instruction:
Write a dark, high-density, multi-syllabic rap piece inspired by Eminem, billy woods, clipping., and Dälek, grounded in the provided chapter descriptions and transcript.

## Requirements

- Vibe: sinister, clinical, oppressive.
- Diction: concrete and cinematic. Harvest specific nouns, settings, objects, and phrases from the transcript. Avoid generic filler entirely.
- Proper nouns: avoid hard-to-pronounce names of people, places, or things. Instead, refer to characters and individuals by their defining role, title, or position within the narrative (e.g., "the warden," "the clerk," "the prisoner" rather than their given name).
- Clarity: intelligible and vivid. Dense, but not word salad. Let images lead the meaning.
- Rhyme:
  - Only use clear and perfect rhymes that are obvious to a basic text to speech system.
  - Internal rhymes are allowed only if they are exact sound matches.
  - No slant rhymes, half rhymes, near rhymes, vowel-shift tricks, or consonant swaps.
  - Multi-syllable rhymes must match exactly across all syllables.
  - Switch patterns across lines. Do not rhyme any word with itself.
  - Do not run the same suffix family for more than two consecutive lines.
- Line openings:
  - Vary the first word of every line. No single word (especially "The") may start more than two lines in any verse or chorus.
  - Choose whatever opener fits the phrasing naturally, but ensure variety — the result should show a mix of articles, pronouns, verbs, prepositions, adjectives, and nouns as opening words, not forced rotation.
  - Consecutive lines should generally avoid sharing the same opening word unless repetition serves a deliberate poetic effect like anaphora.
- Tone tools: controlled alliteration, assonance, caesura, occasional short punch lines against longer lines. No self-referential language about performing or craft terms.
- Forbidden: the word yo; any "rap like" phrasing.

## Structure and format (follow exactly)

- The lyrics field must contain only the lyrics in this exact layout.
- Each Verse must be exactly 16 lines. Each line is a separate string in the array.
- Chorus must be exactly 4 lines. Each line is a separate string in the array.
- Do not include any punctuation or numbers or bold or brackets or parentheses around the words Verse or Chorus (they must appear exactly as plain section headers on their own line).
- Do not include a title inside lyrics.
- Do not add introductions or explanations.

examples.json:
{
  "title": "Track One",
  "verse1": ["line 1", "line 2", "...16 lines total, concrete transcript details, varied end-rhyme families with exact matches, no self-rhymes"],
  "chorus1": ["line 1", "line 2", "line 3", "line 4"],
  "verse2": ["line 1", "line 2", "...16 lines total, escalate stakes and specificity, vary rhyme and cadence, perfect rhymes only"],
  "chorus2": ["line 1", "line 2", "line 3", "line 4"],
  "verse3": ["line 1", "line 2", "...16 lines total, culmination; most vivid and damning imagery from the transcript, perfect rhymes only"],
  "chorus3": ["line 1", "line 2", "line 3", "line 4"]
}
examples.markdown:
## Exact layout to output (use this template verbatim)

Verse

[16 lines, concrete transcript details, varied end-rhyme families with exact matches, no self-rhymes]

Chorus

[4 lines, memorable refrain drawn from transcript language, no clichés, all perfect rhymes]

Verse

[16 lines, escalate stakes and specificity, vary rhyme and cadence, perfect rhymes only]

Chorus

[repeat or evolve the refrain, still 4 lines, perfect rhymes]

Verse

[16 lines, culmination; most vivid and damning imagery from the transcript, perfect rhymes only]

Chorus

[final 4-line refrain, tightened and conclusive, perfect rhymes]
